scheme_name: Gujarat Textile Policy: Assistance to SHG for Job Work
page_title: Gujarat Textile Policy: Assistance to SHG for Job Work

details: The "Gujarat Textile Policy" is an umbrella scheme introduced by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aimed at augmenting investments in the textile sector and strengthening the textile value chain across each sub-sector, while also focusing on strengthening the garments and apparel as well as technical textiles industry. Effective from October 1st, 2024 to September 29th, 2029, this initiative focuses on reducing the carbon footprint and promoting green growth, thereby making the sector globally competitive and environmentally sustainable.

The component "Assistance to SHG for Job Work" provides financial assistance to Self-Help Groups (SHGs) engaged in job work. Eligible SHG members can receive payroll assistance and incentives to enhance their financial stability and encourage women's participation in industrial activities.
benefits: ₹5,000/- per month per member for a period of 3 months
25% of job work value turnover limited to ₹5,000/- per month per member for 5 years
In case, if SHG is independently engaged in manufacturing (not doing job work) as defined under this Resolution, then it will not be eligible for the above incentives.
eligibility: The SHG must have at least 25 female members.
Assistance is available only for female workers of the SHG.
The industrial unit must pay ₹5,000/- per month per female worker in addition to the job work charges.
The SHG should be engaged in job work for industrial units.

offline: The application has to be made to the Industries Commissioner in the
along with the following documents within one year from loan disbursement, production start, or policy operative date (whichever is later).
On receipt of the application and after the scrutiny and verification of relevant documents as per the procedure prescribed, registration certificate will be issued by the Industrial Commissioner.
MSME Units having GFCI up to INR 10 Crore:
After DoCP, the Industrial Unit shall submit an application to the General Manager, District Industries Center.
MSME Units having GFCI above INR 10 Crore and up to 50 Crore:

After DoCP, the Industrial Unit shall submit an application to MSME Commissioner for Provisional Eligibility Certificate within 1 year from DoCP or within one year from the date of issuance of this GR, whichever is later.

After DoCP, the Industrial Unit, having registration shall submit an application for Provisional Eligibility Certificate to the Industries Commissioner within 1 year from DoCP or within 1 year from the date of issuance of the registration certificate, whichever is later.

documents_required: Document of registration of the industrial undertaking, as applicable under law, and the Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum, as prescribed by the Government of India.

Documents related to legal possession of land with valid nonagriculture permission for industrial use, and registered purchase / Lease / Rent deed. If the plot or shed is in GIDC estate, a copy of the possession letter should be attached.
Consent to Establish from GPCB, if applicable.
Detailed Project Report containing the following:
Details of existing business (in case of expansion)
Term loan sanction letter from financial institution(s)
PAN Card of Enterprise and Authorized Person
First Sale Bill ( In case of Commercial Production is commenced)

faqs.question: What is the "Gujarat Textile Policy"?
faqs.answer: The "Gujarat Textile Policy" is a government initiative aimed at strengthening the textile sector by promoting investment, sustainability, employment generation, and value chain integration.

faqs.question: What is the operative period of the "Gujarat Textile Policy"?
faqs.answer: The operative period of the Gujarat Textile Policy 2024 is from October 1, 2024, to September 30, 2029, covering a span of five years.

faqs.question: How does the policy contribute to employment generation?
faqs.answer: The policy focuses on creating a robust textile ecosystem by attracting investments, encouraging MSMEs, supporting women entrepreneurs, and expanding production capacities, thereby generating employment opportunities.

faqs.question: What incentives are provided under the policy?
faqs.answer: The policy offers financial assistance, infrastructure support, and subsidies to strengthen textile manufacturing, encourage investment, and support MSMEs, startups, and large enterprises.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the "Assistance to SHG for Job Work" component?
faqs.answer: The component "Assistance to SHG for Job Work" is a financial assistance program that provides financial support to Self-Help Groups (SHGs) engaged in job work, encouraging women's participation in industrial activities.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this assistance?
faqs.answer: Any SHG with at least 25 female members engaged in job work for an industrial unit is eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What financial assistance can an SHG receive under this scheme?
faqs.answer: An SHG can receive ₹5,000/- per member per month for up to five years, along with 25% of the job work turnover as an incentive.

faqs.question: Is this scheme available for male workers in an SHG?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is available only for female workers of the SHG.

faqs.question: Can an SHG involved in independent manufacturing apply for this assistance?
faqs.answer: No, SHGs engaged in independent manufacturing are not eligible for this assistance.

faqs.question: How is the payroll assistance provided to SHG members?
faqs.answer: The industrial unit pays ₹5,000/- per female worker to the SHG, and the SHG then transfers the amount directly to the members’ bank accounts.

faqs.question: What happens if the industrial unit does not pay ₹5,000/- per member?
faqs.answer: If the industrial unit fails to pay INR 5,000 per female worker, the payroll assistance will not be reimbursed by the government.

faqs.question: For how long can an SHG avail of this assistance?
faqs.answer: The payroll assistance is available for a maximum period of 5 years per member.
